mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3pp increase in the unemployment rate over 3 months signals a weakening labor market, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index over 6 months signals rising inflation expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in rising inflation periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Industrial Production Index over 3 months signals a strengthening supply chain, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening supply chain periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in the Real Estate Sales Index over 6 months signals a strengthening housing market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening housing market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the federal funds rate over 3 months signals an easing monetary cycle, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2pp decrease in the unemployment rate over 3 months signals a strengthening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in strengthening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index and a 1% increase in the Industrial Production Index over 6 months signal rising inflation expectations and a strengthening supply chain, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 2% and INDPRO rises > 1% in 6 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in rising inflation and strengthening supply chain periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
